title: Principles of Physical Hydrogeology
description: Theories of storage and movement of water within groundwater systems; groundwater-surface water interaction; role of groundwater systems in watershed management and regulating the environmental impacts of global change.

title: Climate Change: Causes and Solutions
description: Mechanisms and processes of past and future global climate change. Climate models and projected impacts. Climate solutions including carbon sequestration, adaptation and geoengineering.

title: Climate Change: Evidence and Impacts
description: Climate science fundamentals: energy balance, ocean and atmospheric circulation. Future climate scenarios. Processes driving spatial and temporal climate variability.

title: Geoscientific Data Analysis and Empirical Modelling
description: Application of data analysis and empirical modelling strategies for conducting data-oriented research in geosciences; from setting research questions and hypotheses to delivering and interpreting results.

title: Physical Climate Modelling
description: Understanding climate models from 1D energy balance to fully coupled atmosphere-ocean general circulation models. Physics and thermodynamics of radiative balance and clouds.

title: Climate Measurement and Analysis
description: Local climate time series collection and analysis. Retrieval and analysis of online climate data and model output.

title: Biomathematics
description: Uses of mathematics in the biological sciences; experimental design and modelling of biological processes.

title: Analytical Methods in Sustainability Science
description: Examines sustainability through case studies employing cost-benefit analysis, trade-offs analysis, and life-cycle analysis; active learning in a computer lab using real-world data.

title: Energy and Waves
description: Fluids, harmonic oscillator, travelling waves, standing waves, sound, and interference of light waves, including diffraction.

title: The Climate System
description: Introduction to Earth's climate system: radiation balance, greenhouse effect, atmosphere and ocean circulation, plate tectonics, biosphere interactions, and the carbon cycle.

title: Structure and Bonding in Chemistry
description: Fundamentals of bonding theories and structural chemistry, with applications relevant to modern society.

title: Introduction to Environmental Science
description: Seminars and field trips introducing the major global, regional, and local environmental issues facing human societies.

title: Computation, Programs, and Programming
description: Fundamental program and computation structures. Introductory programming skills. Computation as a tool for information processing, simulation and modelling, and interacting with the world.

title: Software Construction
description: Design, development, and analysis of robust software components. Topics such as software design, computational models, data structures, debugging, and testing.

title: Differential Calculus with Applications
description: Derivatives of elementary functions. Applications and modelling: graphing, optimization.

title: Integral Calculus with Applications
description: The definite integral, integration techniques, applications, modelling, infinite series.

title: Calculus III
description: Analytic geometry in 2 and 3 dimensions, partial and directional derivatives, chain rule, maxima and minima, second derivative test, Lagrange multipliers, multiple integrals with applications.

title: Matrix Algebra
description: Systems of linear equations, operations on matrices, determinants, eigenvalues and eigenvectors, diagonalization of symmetric matrices.

title: Elementary Statistics for Applications
description: Classical, nonparametric, and robust inferences about means, variances, and analysis of variance, using computers. Emphasis on problem formulation, assumptions, and interpretation.
